notes.anchor: this is the first time I have come up to your assembly
notes.note: Pro Lege Manilia (also called De Imperio Cn. Pompei) is Cicero's first surviving political speech, delivered to a contio in support of the lex Manilia, which transferred command in the Mithridatic War to Pompey.
Mithridates VI Eupator of Pontus (c. 134--63 BC), Rome's most enduring foreign adversary; the Mithridatic Wars (88--63 BC) culminated in the campaigns of Lucullus and Pompey.
